cases.prompt: Configure scheduled updates for a manifest with db and web services; web is the visible application and drives versions.
Track a mutable nightly container tag on an ARM64 self-hosted runner.
Use an explicit Docker accelerator and publish only to a MiaoMiao private store.
Build a Go Exec application on Linux ARM64 and publish an x64 LPK from tags.
Build a service-free TypeScript static Web application when a GitHub Release is published.
Review a configuration that enables the official store while using direct delivery.
Configure official and MiaoMiao publishing so an already-online equal package version is not submitted again, and the private app requires group codes.
Inspect this LazyCat repository and automatically create the GitHub Actions files needed for scheduled image updates and release publishing. Do not just explain what I should write.
The versioned GitHub Release LPK already exists. Reconcile an official store that is missing this version while the private store is already current.
Map image tag 20260603.01 to valid SemVer 20260603.1.0 without a repository-specific script.
A MiaoMiao application already exists and is searchable by application name but not package ID. Configure publishing without APP_ID.
Creation-time image sorting picked rebuilt tag 18.0 while package.yml is 19.0.0. Prevent accidental rollback but preserve intentional rollback support.
A Rust and Node source build fails on Tags because protoc is missing, while Ubuntu's protoc rejects Protobuf Edition 2023. Fix automation without changing source semantics.
Configure safe official-store retries and explain how a dual-store workflow handles an official HTTP 400 after the private store succeeds.
Docker Hub updated v1.2.15 after v1.2.26. Prefer the most recently updated tag and target a future arm64 LazyCat device without changing defaults for other repositories.
The official image publishes only latest. Automatically increment the package patch version when its amd64 digest changes, publish both stores, and avoid duplicate releases when it does not change.
Generate a caller-owned composite workflow that checks out the repository and configures Node without GitHub's Node.js 20 deprecation warning.
